what: Remove unnecessary .to(tl.int64) cast in FP8 V load offset computation — max offset fits int32 (256*8192*576+511 < 2^31). Int64 ops are 2x slower on CDNA.
why: Inner-loop int64 offset calculation is a compute bottleneck; int32 arithmetic halves the ALU cost for V loads

what: Use MXFP4 data for V accumulation: dequant first 512 dims from packed FP4 data using tl.dot_scaled with identity-like structure, avoiding separate FP8 V load (saves 512 bytes/token, replaces with ~272 bytes already loaded for K)
why: V load is the dominant bandwidth cost (512 FP8 bytes vs 306 MXFP4 bytes for K). Reusing MXFP4 data for V eliminates redundant memory traffic.

what: Reduce stage2 num_warps from 4 to 1 — stage2 only reads NSPLIT (8 or 16) values and combines them, wastes 3 warps of resources
why: Stage2 is trivially lightweight per workgroup (reads 8-16 floats + 8-16 bf16 vectors). 4 warps per workgroup wastes VGPR and occupancy for no benefit.

what: Use bf16 accumulator for V instead of float32: halves register usage (512 bf16 vs 512 f32 = 1KB vs 2KB). Apply fp8_scale after accumulation to reduce per-element multiply.
why: Register pressure from 512-wide f32 accumulator limits occupancy. bf16 accum with deferred scaling reduces VGPR by ~50% for V path.

what: Aggressive Python overhead reduction: pre-cache tensor data_ptr+strides for view/reshape, inline all constants, minimize dict lookups, pre-bind kernel grid params
why: LB secret shapes likely biased small (geomean 18us). For sub-ms kernels, Python dispatch overhead is significant. Every microsecond of Python host code counts.
override: v124 only cached view by data_ptr; this combines multiple Python-side optimizations including pre-binding and constant inlining

what: BN=128 with num_warps=4 for stage1: halve loop iterations for kv=1024 splits, reduce loop overhead. v115 tried BN=128+warps=8 which failed; this keeps warps=4.
why: For small kv splits (128 tokens), BN=64 means only 2 iterations with high loop overhead. BN=128 means 1 iteration with zero loop overhead.
override: v115 used BN=128+warps=8, this uses BN=128+warps=4 which is a different occupancy tradeoff
